title: SpaceEye-T: 25 cm Satellite Imagery and Tasking Now Available Through Pacific Geomatics Limited
summary: SpaceEye-T is now offering 25 cm satellite imagery and tasking services through Pacific Geomatics Limited for Canadian and international organizations. The service was announced in a collaboration between SpaceEye-T and Pacific Geomatics Limited.

title: SES orders 28 satellites from K2 Space for next-gen MEO network
summary: SES has placed an order for 28 satellites from K2 Space to build its next-generation MEO network, meoSphere, which is expected to be operational by 2030.

title: Fusion engine for missile warning lacks a government dataset
summary: A fusion engine for missile warning is hindered by the lack of shared raw data among government agencies, despite having sensors that could enhance missile-defense capabilities. Northrop Grumman highlights that agencies design their sensors for different purposes, limiting interoperability.

title: Space Force adds cyber units to guard rocket launches
summary: The U.S. Space Force has established cyber defense squadrons at Cape Canaveral and Vandenberg to protect rocket launches from potential cyberattacks. This move highlights increasing concerns about malicious hacking threats in the space industry.

title: Amazon Leo targets faster deployment cadence as deadline pressure mounts
summary: Amazon aims to double its annual launch rate for its LEO broadband constellation to over 20 missions, relying on rockets that are still unproven at scale. The accelerated deployment is driven by increasing deadline pressures.

title: Parsons unveils new satellite antenna aimed at military market
summary: Parsons and Raven Defense have developed a hybrid satellite antenna combining a six-meter parabolic dish with an electronically steered phased-array feed, targeting the military market.

title: Sovereign demand and institutional capital reshape space economy
summary: Institutional capital and sovereign demand are reshaping the space economy, with large infrastructure funds showing interest in the sector. This trend is boosting confidence among early-stage investors to support ambitious startups.

title: Arianespace to launch Katalyst servicing spacecraft
summary: Arianespace will launch a geosynchronous orbit servicing spacecraft named Katalyst for Katalyst Space Technologies aboard an Ariane 6 rocket in 2027. This mission marks a significant step in in-space servicing and orbital maintenance.

title: York Space rides defense demand as procurement changes cloud outlook
summary: York Space Systems is leveraging increased defense demand and commercial contracts to navigate changes in procurement driven by the Space Development Agency. However, shifts in agency-led programs may cloud its long-term outlook.

title: The software behind Artemis, Gateway and America’s defense in space deserves its own strategy
summary: The article highlights the overlooked importance of software in national space infrastructure, including missions like Artemis and Gateway, and argues for a dedicated strategy to address it.

title: Watch all your favorite sci-fi content like 'For All Mankind' and 'Star City' anywhere with this exclusive deal and get a free $50 Amazon Gift Card and 77% off NordVPN
summary: An exclusive deal offers discounts on NordVPN and an Amazon gift card, allowing users to stream sci-fi content like 'For All Mankind' securely while traveling.

title: Hubble revisits the Crab Nebula after 25 years | Space photo of the day for March 24, 2026
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has captured new images of the Crab Nebula, revisiting the iconic celestial object 25 years after its first observation. The updated photos provide fresh insights into the nebula's structure and evolution.

title: Arriving safely on Venus and Titan: Different atmospheres affect heat shields differently, study shows
summary: A study reveals that different planetary atmospheres, such as those of Venus and Titan, affect heat shields on spacecraft differently during entry. This highlights the need for tailored heat shield designs for various planetary missions.

title: The best Amazon Big Spring Sale deals for 2026: Sizeable savings on drones, Lego, streaming, star projectors and much more
summary: Amazon's Big Spring Sale for 2026 offers discounts on space and sci-fi tech, including star projectors and drones. The sale also includes deals on Lego and streaming services.

title: Get one last look at the Pleiades before it disappears from the spring sky
summary: The Pleiades star cluster is about to disappear from the spring sky as it sets with the sun. Stargazers are advised on how to spot it before it fades into the evening glow.

title: NASA announcing update to Artemis moon plans today: Watch it live
summary: NASA is announcing updates to its Artemis moon mission plans today, with live events scheduled for March 24. The updates will cover the agency's future lunar exploration strategies.

title: Scientists find 2 'failed stars' that may have a second chance to shine bright — by getting together
summary: Scientists discovered that two 'failed stars' (brown dwarfs) could potentially merge and reignite into a new star. This rare collision might give them a second chance to shine brightly.

title: 10 major differences between the 'Project Hail Mary' book and movie
summary: The article compares 10 major differences between Andy Weir's 'Project Hail Mary' book and its upcoming movie adaptation. The story follows Ryland Grace's world-saving adventure, which underwent several changes from page to screen.

title: 2nd-ever test flight of NASA's 'quiet' X-59 supersonic jet cut short by glitch
summary: NASA's X-59 'quiet' supersonic jet completed its second test flight on March 20, but the flight was cut short due to a technical glitch.

title: NASA Research Proposes Technology to Seek Earth-Like Exoplanets
summary: NASA is advancing technologies to locate and explore Earth-like exoplanets by observing reflected light, which may reveal indicators like water and oxygen. This research aims to understand the mysteries of the universe and identify potential habitable worlds beyond our solar system.

title: NASA’s Water-Hunting Tool Will Help Scout Moon’s South Pole
summary: NASA is contributing a water-detecting instrument, the Neutron Spectrometer System (NSS), to the Lunar Polar Exploration (LUPEX) mission led by JAXA and ISRO to scout for ice on the Moon's south pole for future human exploration.

title: NASA Unveils Initiatives to Achieve America’s National Space Policy
summary: NASA announced transformative initiatives during its 'Ignition' event to align with President Trump's National Space Policy and enhance U.S. leadership in space. The initiatives aim to drive world-changing science and discovery.

title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle Crosses Australia
summary: Tropical Cyclone Narelle impacted northern Australia with strong winds and heavy rainfall as it made multiple landfalls. The storm caused significant damage along the coast.

title: NASA Data Hackathon Inspires Community Action
summary: NASA hosted an Environmental Monitoring through Education, Research, and Geospatial Engagement (EMERGE) Data Hackathon at the University of Florida, engaging students, researchers, and community members in environmental and public health science using NASA data tools.

title: See NASA’s GUARDIAN Catch a Tsunami
summary: NASA's experimental GUARDIAN technology uses satellite navigation signals to detect tsunamis and provide early warnings to at-risk communities. The GNSS Upper Atmospheric Real-time Disaster Information and Alert Network (GUARDIAN) identifies slight distortions in signals to spot hazards in real time.

title: SWOT Mission Unlocks a New View of Our Waterways
summary: The SWOT mission has provided new insights into Earth's waterways, revealing how rivers move, change, and support life. This data enhances understanding of global water systems and their ecological importance.

title: NASA’s Hubble, Webb Telescopes Survey Pinwheel Galaxy
summary: NASA's Hubble and James Webb Space Telescopes captured a detailed image of the Pinwheel Galaxy (Messier 101) in March 2026. The galaxy, located 25 million light-years away, is one of the closest face-on spiral galaxies to Earth.

title: NASA to Provide Update on Implementation of National Space Policy
summary: NASA will host a public event to discuss its implementation of President Trump's National Space Policy. The update will take place at NASA Headquarters in Washington on March 24, 2026.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on the construction of launch pads at Starbase in preparation for the upcoming Block 3 Starships. This activity highlights SpaceX's efforts to increase launch frequency.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the United States will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V. The launches are scheduled across multiple countries including China, New Zealand, and Norway.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 successfully launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from the repaired Baikonur Cosmodrome pad. The mission follows a previous launch delay due to pad repairs.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center in space.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the planned lunar flyby mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. This brings the next Starship launch one step closer to realization.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of launches is scheduled with nine missions planned, including launches by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin has introduced NEO Hunter, a new mission concept aimed at planetary defense against near-Earth objects. The hybrid system combines various technologies to detect and potentially deflect hazardous asteroids.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans for lunar and Martian missions, transforming its Starfactory facility to produce Starship rockets. The company is repurposing the facility from steel production to spacecraft manufacturing.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: The International Space Station (ISS) welcomed new crew members in February following the early departure of Crew-11 in mid-January due to a medical concern. The month was marked by new crew arrivals and ongoing scientific research aboard the ISS.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the event, company, or category were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. This milestone reflects the agency's significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the CM25 Council Meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in ESA's Large Space Simulator. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center in the Netherlands. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will introduce ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference while being cost-effective. The innovation aims to replace bulky and expensive antennas with a compact and affordable alternative.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments. This initiative aims to support research in space technology and microgravity conditions.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio Hosted Payload Platform
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio hosted payload platform, a new model where satellites stay in orbit while payloads can be added or removed as needed. This innovation aims to enhance flexibility and efficiency in space missions.

title: Ramon.Space, Ingrasys Aim To Fly Prototype Orbital Data Center In 2027
summary: Ramon.Space and Ingrasys plan to launch a prototype orbital data center by 2027, joining the growing competition in space-based data processing. The initiative aims to leverage space environments for advanced computing applications.

title: Beyond the Rocket: The Digital Infrastructure of the Artemis II Mission
summary: The Artemis II mission relies on advanced digital infrastructure beyond the visible rocket, marking a significant technological leap from Apollo. NASA and its partners, including Booz Allen, emphasize the critical role of these unseen systems in mission success.

title: Contrivian Launches New Product Bundling Amazon Leo & Starlink
summary: Contrivian has launched a new product that bundles satellite connectivity services from Amazon's Project Kuiper (Leo) and SpaceX's Starlink, allowing customers to use both networks seamlessly. This offering eliminates the need for customers to choose between providers.

title: SpaceX v. Amazon Heats Up at the FCC
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are engaged in a regulatory dispute at the FCC, with both companies publicly challenging each other's satellite constellations. The conflict highlights the competitive tensions in the growing satcom industry.

title: High Fire Volume Systems: An Alternative to Space-Based Interceptors
summary: The Pentagon's Golden Dome program faces challenges with traditional space-based interceptors, leading to exploration of alternative approaches like deep magazine systems and directed energy. These alternatives aim to address engineering, procurement, and scaling limitations.

title: Kayhan Space Launches Satcat Terminal
summary: Kayhan Space has launched a new Satcat Terminal business, offering tools for space situational awareness. This development is notable for its potential impact on space traffic management.

title: Inside Austria’s Small But Mighty Space Ecosystem
summary: Austria's space industry is experiencing growth with increased funding and startup activity, marking a renaissance in its ecosystem.

title: Portal, Paladin Team On Debris Removal Service
summary: Portal and Paladin are collaborating on an operational debris removal service, emphasizing the importance of active debris management for critical satellite infrastructure. Portal CEO Jeff Thornburg highlights the shift from experimental to practical solutions in maintaining space-based services.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its SR75 suborbital rocket, with the second flight scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Amazon Leo Readies 200+ Satellites for Orbit as It Ramps Up Launch Schedule
summary: Amazon's Leo project has prepared over 200 satellites for launch at Cape Canaveral, with multiple launches planned in the coming months to expand its satellite constellation.

title: Space Force, Industry Leaders Warn of Critical Gaps in Orbital Intelligence
summary: The U.S. Space Force and industry leaders highlight significant gaps in orbital intelligence despite advanced tracking systems like ground radars and GEO spy satellites. These gaps pose risks to space domain awareness and security.

title: Interview With TE Connectivity Principal Engineer Bruce Conway at SATShow Week
summary: TE Connectivity's Principal Engineer Bruce Conway discusses the company's interconnect solutions for satellites and solar panels, highlighting its deep space heritage during SATShow Week.

title: Seraphim CEO: Space Investment is at a ‘Pivotal Time’
summary: Seraphim Space CEO Mark Boggett highlighted that space investment is at a critical juncture, describing it as a 'pivotal time' for the industry during a keynote at SATELLITE 2026.

title: Space Compass Purchases First Optical Data Relay Satellite From Swissto12
summary: Space Compass has purchased its first optical data relay satellite from Swissto12 for a planned GEO constellation. The Japanese telecommunications firm aims to enhance data relay capabilities.

title: Kymeta Advances Multi-Band, Multi-Orbit Antenna Toward 2027 Release
summary: Kymeta is developing the KuKa 8 Series, a multi-band, multi-orbit single aperture user terminal, with first prototypes expected this year. The technology is slated for a 2027 release.

title: Interview With MPG Business Development Manager Dave Rawlinson at SATShow Week
summary: MPG is addressing accelerating pacing threats for government and commercial customers by scaling up its operations to meet unprecedented demand. The company's Business Development Manager, Dave Rawlinson, discussed this during an interview at SATShow Week.

title: China’s Space Development is Outpacing Expectations and Gaining Momentum, Space Force Official Says
summary: China's space development is advancing rapidly, surpassing expectations and outpacing Russia, prompting increased U.S. analysis. A Space Force official highlights China's progress toward becoming a major space power.

title: Spire Targets 50% Growth in 2026 After Adjustments in 2025
summary: Spire Global aims for 50% revenue growth in 2026 after divesting its maritime business and reducing debt in 2025, according to CEO Theresa Condor.

title: Eutelsat CEO Fallacher Talks OneWeb Gen 2, Global Competition, and Sovereign Demand
summary: Eutelsat Group CEO Jean-François Fallacher discusses the company's strategic focus on OneWeb Gen 2, global competition, and sovereign demand amid its ongoing transformation. The interview highlights Eutelsat's evolving role in the satellite industry.
